mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly suppressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing immediate negative equity returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when unemployment spikes exceed the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and economic momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint increases in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive short-term equity returns within 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 5-day returns during recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining inflation reduces real interest rate pressure and improves corporate profit margins, generating positive forward returns over the next month when price growth falls below the 2.5% annual threshold.
failure_condition: Equity returns turn negative when inflation drops below the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling total employment and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness, triggering negative equity returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The employment-unemployment divergence fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth expands corporate earnings expectations and improves risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no correlation with 60-day forward returns or yields negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in economic activity and supply chain stress, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Consecutive industrial production declines are followed by positive 20-day returns.
